own_review.point: Per-directory guard `command[0].startsWith(binDirectory + "/")` (ProcessRunner, DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition) and `executable.startsWith(binDirectory)` (CredentialHelper) is flawed: a command already qualified with one bin dir (or any absolute path) passes the guard on the OTHER iteration, producing malformed retries like '/usr/local/bin//opt/homebrew/bin/docker'. Maintainer polish (1519bcf1e23) rewrote it to `startsWith("/")` in two files and deleted the check in CredentialHelper; the human reviewer separately asked to revert these guards as unrelated/unnecessary (commands never contain '/').

DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition fallback loop swallows failures with `catch (Exception ignored) {}`, losing diagnostics and inconsistent with the other two classes which addSuppressed; polish changed it to ex.addSuppressed(suppressed).

CredentialHelper.start mutates the shared ProcessBuilder across loop iterations: second iteration copies the already-mutated command and only works because index 0 is overwritten with the pre-captured executable; fragile. Polish snapshots the original command via List.copyOf before the loop. Also the list copy + startsWith check inside try before `continue` is dead work.
CredentialHelperTests: two `anySatisfy` assertions are weaker than asserting exactly the two expected suppressed exceptions; polish replaced with satisfiesExactlyInAnyOrder.

Behavioral notes (acceptable): PATH semantics now retry when PATH has /usr/local/bin but lacks /opt/homebrew/bin (intended); ProcessRunner fallback no longer recurses, which actually improves error reporting (original IOException preserved with suppressed instead of being replaced by the fallback's ProcessStartException).

points.point: Flawed continue guard: absolute/already-prefixed command gets the other directory prepended, yielding malformed paths; fix with startsWith("/")
points.why: Verified; exactly what the maintainer polish fixed and the human flagged; even correctly notes it is unreachable for CredentialHelper (executable is 'docker-credential-'+name).

points.point: No tests cover ProcessRunner macOS fallback (runWhenProcessDoesNotStart only asserts exception type)
points.why: Verified against base ProcessRunnerTests.java:52 — assertion-only, no suppressed-path checks.

points.point: No tests cover DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition fallback (existing test is a smoke test)
points.why: Verified: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableTests is a single @DisabledIfProcessUnavailable smoke test.

points.point: Inconsistent trailing-slash convention across the three files' constants
points.why: True but pre-existing per-file convention for USR_LOCAL_BIN; cosmetic.

points.point: Three files duplicate constants/loop; consider shared utility
points.why: Modules are intentionally independent (buildpack/core/test-support); not actionable here.

missed: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition swallows fallback exceptions with catch(ignored) instead of addSuppressed (polish fixed)
CredentialHelper mutating the shared ProcessBuilder across iterations is fragile (polish snapshots original command)
Weak anySatisfy test assertion vs exact two suppressed exceptions (polish used satisfiesExactlyInAnyOrder)
exceeds: Missing fallback test coverage in ProcessRunnerTests and DisabledIfProcessUnavailableTests — verified, raised by neither human nor judge

points.point: Trailing-slash inconsistency between CredentialHelper and the other two files
CredentialHelper executable capture is 'correct and necessary... sound'; ProcessRunner pattern slightly cleaner
points.why: Blesses the fragile mutation as sound; only a soft style aside, no actionable finding.

points.point: allSatisfy->anySatisfy change is correct, test well-formed
points.why: Praise; polish actually strengthened this assertion.
False: this guard is precisely the flaw — cross-directory prefixing of absolute commands; human asked to revert it and polish rewrote it to startsWith("/").

points.point: Overall: consistent conventions, no regressions, low risk

missed: Cross-prefix guard bug / human's 'revert unnecessary startsWith guards' theme (actively asserted the opposite)

points.point: CredentialHelper mutation-in-loop is fragile; copies previously mutated list, works accidentally; snapshot original command or fresh ProcessBuilder
points.why: Verified, mechanics exactly right; matches maintainer polish (List.copyOf originalCommand).

points.point: ProcessRunner's fresh-ProcessBuilder approach is the model to follow
Add count assertion (hasSize(2)) since anySatisfy doesn't pin suppressed count
points.why: Verified; polish replaced with satisfiesExactlyInAnyOrder, achieving exactly this.

missed: Cross-prefix guard bug / human's 'revert unnecessary startsWith guards' theme (summary even calls the changes 'functionally correct')

qualitative: v2 is clearly the best: it alone found the cross-directory prefixing bug in the per-directory startsWith guards — the exact code the human reviewer asked to revert and that the maintainer's polish commit rewrote to startsWith("/") — and it even correctly scoped reachability (unreachable in CredentialHelper since executables are 'docker-credential-'+name). Its missing-test-coverage points were verified and exceed both human and judge findings, though it missed the swallowed-exception and ProcessBuilder-mutation issues. v4 is solid on the secondary issues (mutation fragility and weak test assertion, both matching the polish) but missed the headline guard bug entirely and declared the PR functionally correct. v3 is the weakest: almost entirely praise, it explicitly endorsed the flawed guard as 'correct... Well done' — a false verification of the one defect the maintainers had to fix — and blessed the fragile mutation pattern and the weak test assertion as sound.
